steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Triangle LMN has LM = x, MN = x, and NL = y. The three sides sum to x + x + y = 2x + y.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The equation 2x + y = 49 equates the perimeter to 49. So 49 is the sum of the lengths of all three sides.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: This matches the definition of perimeter: the total distance around the triangle.

choiceElimination:
• Choice A — The difference, in centimeters, between the lengths of sides $LM$ and $NL$: The equation adds all sides, not subtracts any two. ✗
  • Choice B — The difference, in centimeters, between the lengths of sides $LM$ and $MN$: LM and MN are both x, so their difference is 0, not 49. ✗
  • Choice C — The sum of the lengths, in centimeters, of the three sides of the triangle: 2x + y = x + x + y = LM + MN + NL = perimeter = 49. ✓
  • Choice D — The length, in centimeters, of one of the two sides of equal length: That would be x, not 49, which is the full sum. ✗
C is correct.
